title: Chrozophora obliquifolia
taxonomy: Chrozophora obliquifolia is classified within the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the spurge family. This family consists of a diverse group of flowering plants, including trees, shrubs, and herbs. Chrozophora obliquifolia is a species of flowering plant that is native to the Mediterranean region. The species is characterized by its distinctive leaves and ability to produce toxic milky sap. As a member of the Euphorbiaceae family, Chrozophora obliquifolia shares characteristics with other spurge family members, such as the presence of milky sap and compound leaves.
